channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lessons from the 1953 CIA Regime Change in Iran and Trump's Lack of a Clear Strategy
item.description: The article compares the U.S. bombing of Iran in 2026 with the CIA's covert operation in 1953 that successfully ousted Iran's Prime Minister Mohammad Mossadegh. The Trump administration's 2026 militar...

item.title: Philippine Authorities Confirm Termination of Chinese-Linked Espionage Network
item.description: The Philippines’ National Security Council (NSC) announced on March 4, 2026, that espionage operations connected to China have been identified, addressed, and terminated. These operations involved Chi...

item.title: Comelec Suspends Overseas Voter Registration in Israel Until March 7, Iran Until Further Notice Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – The Commission on Elections (Comelec) announced the temporary suspension of overseas voter registration in Israel and Iran due to escalating tensions and ongoing conflict in the ...

item.title: Current Tensions Between the US and UK Amid Iran and Epstein Controversies
item.description: The Sky News podcast highlights growing tensions between the United States and the United Kingdom, particularly focusing on the political dynamic between former President Donald Trump and Prime Minist...

item.title: DNC Cancels Midterm Convention Due to Fundraising Challenges
item.description: The Democratic National Committee (DNC) has announced that it will not be hosting a midterm convention, citing significant fundraising difficulties as the main reason. The decision was made after feed...

item.title: Tinus Greyling's Recovery: A Community's Support in His Fight for Independence
item.description: Tinus Greyling, a 35-year-old from Pretoria, was paralysed from the chest down after a sudden onset of Transverse Myelitis in August 2025. This rare neurological condition, triggered by an autoimmune ...

item.title: Bank of Tanzania Explains Dominance of Two Leading Banks and Sector Oversight
item.description: Tanzania has over 40 banks, but just two of them control more than half of the industry’s assets and profits. Bank of Tanzania Governor Emmanuel Tutuba says this dominance comes from strategic choices...

item.title: Iran Shows Tentative Willingness to Discuss Peace with US Amid Ongoing Conflict
item.description: According to a report by the New York Times on Wednesday, operatives from Iran’s Ministry of Intelligence have indicated a willingness to engage in talks with the US Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) ...

item.title: Learners in Durban found with knives, drugs, and even 'love potions' in anti-crime school searches
item.description: Anti-crime searches conducted at several schools in Durban's north have uncovered alarming items in students' bags, including knives, drugs, and an air gun pellet. The initiative, led by the eThekwini...

item.title: Senators urge DepEd to prioritize 180 school days amid proposed three-term calendar
item.description: During a Senate hearing on March 3, 2026, lawmakers pushed the Department of Education (DepEd) to prioritize the 180 school days required by law, despite proposals to restructure the academic year int...

item.title: Reviewing Nicholas Kaufman's Defense of Duterte at the ICC
item.description: In late February, the International Criminal Court in The Hague hosted the first defense arguments on behalf of former Philippine President Rodrigo Roa Duterte, presented by lead counsel Nicholas Kauf...

item.title: 42-year-old man dies in hospital following head-on collision with fuel tanker near Yass
item.description: A 42-year-old man has tragically died in Canberra Hospital after being involved in a head-on collision with a fuel tanker north of the Australian Capital Territory. The crash occurred on February 19 o...

item.title: Soldier Allegedly Kills YABATECH Student in Lagos During Security Operation
item.description: A Nigerian soldier is accused of fatally shooting Balogun Sultan, a National Diploma II student of Yaba College of Technology (YABATECH), during a security operation in Lagos. Sultan, who was enrolled...

item.title: Three suspects arrested in Westville vehicle theft syndicate
item.description: Three suspects linked to a vehicle hijacking syndicate operating across Westville, Pinetown, Chatsworth, and Malvern were arrested after a high-speed chase near the Pavilion mall. Police and Mobi Claw...

item.title: UK to suspend study and work visas from four countries amid visa misuse concerns
item.description: The UK government has announced that it will cease issuing study visas to nationals from Afghanistan, Cameroon, Myanmar, and Sudan, citing widespread abuse of the visa system. Home Secretary Shabana M...

item.title: UEFA Sanctions Tottenham After Fans Make Nazi Salutes During Champions League Match
item.description: UEFA don fine Tottenham Hotspur and give the club one-year suspended ban from selling away tickets after three of their supporters make Nazi salutes during Champions League match against Eintracht Fra...

item.title: Over 30,000 people affected as water supply is cut in Nyeri, Nyandarua, and Laikipia counties
item.description: Residents of Nyeri, Nyandarua, and Laikipia counties are facing severe water shortages after the Water Resource Authority (WRA) disconnected the supply from the Matitu Water Project. The disconnection...

item.title: UK Organises First Repatriation Flight from Oman for British Nationals Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: The UK government has arranged its first official repatriation flight from Muscat, Oman, for British citizens wishing to leave the Middle East due to escalating regional tensions. The flight, schedule...

item.title: PM urges leaders to continue educating citizens on land management and peace
item.description: Prime Minister Dr. Mwigulu Nchemba has urged local MPs and district leaders in Manyara Region, specifically from Mbulu, to continue educating citizens on land management to prevent conflicts and promo...

item.title: Israeli Strike in Qom Leaves Iranian Clerics Vulnerable Amid Khamenei Succession Uncertainty
item.description: An Israeli airstrike targeted a meeting location in Qom, Iran, where senior clerics of the Assembly of Experts were reportedly planning to discuss succession plans following the death of Supreme Leade...
